title: Tephroseris koreana
taxonomy: Tephroseris koreana is classified within the family Asteraceae, which includes daisies and sunflowers. It is part of the genus Tephroseris, which comprises several species of flowering plants. Tephroseris koreana is a type of alpine plant native to the mountainous regions of Korea. The plant's classification also includes its order (Asterales) and class (Magnoliopsida). Further classification includes the division (Magnoliophyta) and kingdom (Plantae).
uses: Tephroseris koreana is a plant that has been traditionally used in medicine in its native Korea. Its medicinal properties are attributed to its roots, which are said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is also believed to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage. Tephroseris koreana is thought to be used to treat various health conditions, including fever, sore throats, and digestive issues. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential benefits.

intro: Tephroseris koreana, also known as the Korean land snail flower or Korean mountain land snail flower, is a perennial plant species in the family Asteraceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, making it a promising natural remedy for various ailments. The bioactive constituents of Tephroseris koreana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional Korean medicine, Tephroseris koreana is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used to treat fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
